intro: OpenAI's new model family debuts under an unprecedented government-gated rollout; Bitcoin logs its worst ETF-outflow month ever as the EU's MiCA clock ticks to Tuesday's hard deadline.

items.headline: GitHub Copilot for Jira reaches general availability
items.summary: GitHub's coding agent now streams live progress updates directly into Jira issues and accepts follow-up instructions from the ticket's chat panel without switching to GitHub. GA adds model selection, Confluence context via MCP, custom agents, per-space guidance, and a faster setup flow over the March public preview.
items.why: Closes the loop between issue creation and pull-request delivery — the coding agent now lives entirely inside the project-management tool.

items.headline: OpenCode surpasses 160K GitHub stars and 7.5 million monthly developers
items.summary: The open-source, terminal-native coding agent from the SST/Anomaly team has become the most-adopted open-source AI coding agent, connecting to 75+ model providers through a single CLI with full LSP integration and multi-agent support.
items.why: Model-agnostic, self-hosted, MIT-licensed — its growth signals a large segment of developers moving away from proprietary IDE-coupled tools.

items.headline: OpenAI previews GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna under US government-restricted access
items.summary: OpenAI launched a three-tier GPT-5.6 family on June 26 — Sol (flagship, 1.5M-token context, max-reasoning and agentic ultra modes), Terra (GPT-5.5 quality at half the price), and Luna (fast, low-cost) — restricting initial API access to roughly 20 government-approved companies. General availability is expected in coming weeks.
items.why: The first frontier model launch gated by US government approval; Sol's gains in biology and cybersecurity appear to be the reason for the restriction.

items.headline: Node.js moves to one major release per year starting with Node 27
items.summary: The Node.js project is dropping its two-major-versions-per-year cadence: starting with Node 27 in October 2026, one major version ships each year and every release becomes LTS, eliminating the odd/even distinction. Node 26.4.0, the last under the old schedule, shipped June 24.
items.why: Simpler upgrade planning for teams in production — every Node version now carries multi-year support from day one.

items.headline: Bitcoin near $60K as June spot-ETF outflows hit a record $3.6 billion
items.summary: U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs posted $3.6 billion in net outflows through June 26, the largest monthly withdrawal since launch, driven by a PCE inflation print of 4.1% — a three-year high — that pushed rate-cut expectations to late 2026. Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) made its first net BTC sale since December 2022 (32 coins), adding to sentiment pressure.
items.why: Macro headwinds are now clearly outweighing the structural ETF-inflow tailwind that drove BTC's 2025 rally.

items.headline: MiCA enforcement begins Tuesday: most EU-registered crypto firms still unauthorized
items.summary: The EU's MiCA transitional period expires July 1 with no extension, per ESMA. Of 3,167 previously registered VASPs, only 216 have obtained CASP authorization; the rest must cease serving EU clients immediately or face national-regulator enforcement.
items.why: Effective in days for 450 million EU residents — the biggest structural reshaping of European crypto markets since stablecoin rules took effect.
